event: Facade of St. Mark's Basilica, Venice
symbols: golden horses — the four bronze horses of St. Mark's, ancient trophies of conquest
ornate facade — the wealth and devotion of Venice dedicated to its patron saint
description: A painting showing the upper portion of the facade of St. Mark's Basilica in Venice. The composition focuses on the central arch with its elaborate Gothic tracery, sculptures, and golden ornamentation. Golden figures (the famous horses of St. Mark's) are visible on the balustrade. The facade is rendered in grey-white stone with gold highlights against a bright blue sky. The style is painterly and atmospheric, capturing the play of light on the ornate architectural details.
